title: ehretia serrata var. pyrifolia
intro: Ehretia serrata var. pyrifolia, also known as the Wild Orange or East Indian Ehretia, is a plant species belonging to the family Boraginaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial qualities, which are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Ehretia serrata var. pyrifolia include its potential to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its use in treating skin conditions and wounds. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are often used in traditional medicine practices.
